hd_catalog.spectral_type_source: HD Catalog (Cannon & Pickering); confirmed by Gaia logg=3.85
hd_catalog.note: IV/V subgiant classification independently confirms evolved-star nature

tess_spoc_contamination.crowdsap_note: CROWDSAP = fraction of flux from target; 1-CROWDSAP = contamination fraction
tess_spoc_contamination.exofop_note: ExoFOP SPOC pipeline contamination ratio for the 2-min aperture

notes: Gaia RVS RV is a mean over 13 transits; single-epoch precision only, not a mass constraint.
Gaia GSP-Phot Teff (6061 K) differs from TIC Stassun+2019 Teff (6332 K) by 271 K.
Gaia logg=3.85 independently confirms subgiant nature despite TIC lumclass=DWARF.
HD spectral type F7IV/V further supports evolved classification.
No RAVE DR6 match within 5 arcsec (programmatically confirmed 2026-07-09).
No GALAH DR3 match within 5 arcsec (programmatically confirmed 2026-07-09).
No LAMOST DR10 match within 5 arcsec (programmatically confirmed 2026-07-09; target Dec=-53.7 deg outside Northern footprint).
No APOGEE DR17 match within 5 arcsec (programmatically confirmed 2026-07-09; target not in any APOGEE field).
CONCLUSION: No major spectroscopic survey has observed TIC 81077799. All stellar parameters are photometric.
The a/Rstar tension cannot be resolved with existing public data; new dedicated spectroscopy is required.
